title: Best Practices for Project Management in Monday.com
excerpt: “What are the most effective project management strategies you’ve implemented using Monday.com?”

posts.author: davidmiller (David Millar)
posts.cookedHtml: “What are the most effective project management strategies you’ve implemented using Monday.com?”

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi David,

I think there’s no best practice as it depends on each specific use case. What I recommend is to specify the workflow that works best for you and then set it in monday.com. So actually, you define the best practice according to your needs. monday.com is super flexible platform and you will be able to configure it to suite your needs.
If you could share your use case l’m sure the community members here will help you find a solution using monday.com

Oz

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @davidmiller

Being a reputable monday.com consulting company, our developers follow scrum methodology and boards during complex initiatives like app development. This project management strategy helps us to create an app development roadmap easily, run sprints, and review or refine products before launch. In short, the Scrum strategy enables our experts to manage the app development lifecycle by breaking it into short-term iterative sprints. This approach helps in reducing the development complexity and accelerating product launch.

To know more about our development and project management approach, connect with our consultants today!

posts.author: digitalgeek (digitalgeek)
posts.cookedHtml:
One of the best strategies I’ve used with Monday.com is creating custom workflows that align with each project phase — planning, execution, and review. Automations for task assignments and deadline reminders reduce a lot of manual follow-ups.

I’ve also connected dashboards with performance metrics to get quick insights on regional campaigns. For example, while tracking results for “digital marketing in Jalandhar”, the board view made it much easier to compare progress and adjust timelines.

I’ve also found that integrating communication tools into the boards reduces back-and-forth emails and keeps all updates transparent. These small adjustments made collaboration smoother and delivery timelines more predictable.
